clinical.mechanism: Dysregulation of the HPA axis induces a state of chronic hypocortisolemia accompanied by elevations in tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-a) and interleukins. This pro-inflammatory milieu downregulates mitochondrial ATP synthase, leading to severe cellular bioenergetic exhaustion.
protocol: Implement structured pacing protocols, strictly maintaining a fixed daily energy budget.
Integrate low-impact restorative training (Yoga Nidra, NSDR) to rebuild parasympathetic tone.
Check full thyroid panels and early morning cortisol levels to rule out endocrine pathology.
citations: Frontiers in Endocrinology: 'HPA Axis Dysfunction in Chronic Fatigue Syndrome' (2022).
Mitochondrion Journal: 'Cellular Bioenergetics and Mitochondrial Dysfunction'.

witty.metaphorText: Your body is an older house that was wired for 100 amps, but you've plugged in an industrial pottery kiln, a commercial laundry mat, and four crypto-mining rigs.
witty.visualWarehouseDescription: Imagine the electrical board in your basement. Sparks are flying out of the plaster. The main safety breaker is melting. Rather than turning off the commercial dryer (your stressful job), you are running around plugging in extension cords (caffeine pills) trying to power a hot tub. The main system eventually snaps off, leaving you in total pitch blackness, wondering why the ceiling fan won't spin.
witty.wittyAdvice: Caffeine is not energy. It is an interest-bearing loan taken out against your future nervous system. Your credit is currently maxed out.

brutal.realityCheck: You are physically exhausted because you are exhausting to be around. You spend 90% of your energy maintaining a perfect facade of competency while suppressing massive oceans of anger. Your body didn't break down; it staged a mutiny to save your life from your own choices.
protocolSteps: Identify the one commitment you dread every single week, and cancel it immediately.
Stop drinking caffeine after 11 AM. You are whipping an exhausted horse and wondering why it collapses.
Lie flat on the floor in absolute silence for 20 minutes. No phone, no podcast, no planning.
Acknowledge that your need to be needed is what is actually murdering your adrenals.

biologyPath.title: Boundary Failure
biologyPath.description: You say 'Yes' to demands that actually make your soul contract, draining structural limits.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Chronic over-giving and lack of healthy boundaries force your body to constantly produce compensatory adrenaline to survive the day.

biologyPath.title: Adrenal Sprint
biologyPath.description: Your system floods itself with adrenaline and cortisol to push through the exhaustion.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: The body utilizes emergency reserves of glucocorticoids. You feel wired but tired, running on high-friction survival chemicals.

biologyPath.title: HPA Blunting
biologyPath.description: The feedback loop of your stress response system degrades from constant activation.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: The hypothalamus-pituitary-adrenal axis flattens its baseline rhythm, leaving you with extremely low morning cortisol and zero wakefulness signals.

biologyPath.title: Mitochondrial Drag
biologyPath.description: Your cellular powerhouses fail to generate ATP efficiently due to low-grade inflammation.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Low cortisol allows pro-inflammatory cytokines to circulate freely, disrupting mitochondrial membrane potential and rendering normal cellular energy production sluggish.

biologyPath.title: System Shutdown
biologyPath.description: Your body throws the emergency breaker, plunging you into deep, heavy fatigue.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Because you refused to rest willingly, your somatic intelligence cuts the power to force compliance. The bed is your only remaining option.

clinical.label: Strictly Medical
clinical.title: Low Blood Pressure, Orthostatic Symptoms & Shock Screen

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Low Is Not Always Bad
body: Low blood pressure can be normal in some people if there are no symptoms.
The problem starts when flow is not enough for the brain and organs. Very inconsiderate of gravity.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Symptoms and Causes
body: Symptoms can include dizziness, fainting, fatigue, blurred vision, nausea, cold clammy skin, weakness, confusion, or falls.
Causes include dehydration, bleeding, infection, medications, heart problems, endocrine disorders, autonomic dysfunction, and posture-related drops.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Treatment Direction
body: Care may include hydration, salt adjustment if medically appropriate, compression, medication review, treating infection/bleeding/endocrine/heart causes, and positional strategies.
Do not increase salt or fluids aggressively if you have heart/kidney issues without clinician guidance. Bodies love exceptions, because why be simple.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: Seek urgent care for fainting, shock signs, chest pain, shortness of breath, confusion, severe weakness, bleeding, dehydration, fever/infection, or injury from falls.
Passing out is not a productivity strategy.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Tired Pump
paragraphs: Your circulation is a tired pump trying to push blood uphill while gravity acts smug.
Sometimes that is just your normal baseline. Sometimes it is dehydration, medication, autonomic dysfunction, bleeding, infection, heart trouble, or the body running on fumes.
If you stand and the world fades out, sit down. Revolutionary, I know.
Find the cause, treat the volume/pump/tone problem, and stop letting the floor catch you.
Prime the pump safely or keep treating fainting like an inconvenient hobby. Your choice.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest

Medical first: symptomatic hypotension can come from dehydration, bleeding, infection, medication effects, heart issues, endocrine problems, autonomic dysfunction, or shock.
Symbolically, ask where your system has no push left.
But do not romanticize depletion. Fainting and shock signs need care, not poetry.
Prime the pump safely or keep treating fainting like an inconvenient hobby. Your choice.

reset.title: Tired Pump & Orthostatic Safety Reset

Elevate legs if safe and appropriate.
Check for red flags: chest pain, shortness of breath, confusion, bleeding, fever, severe weakness, injury, dehydration, or shock signs.
If red flags are present, seek urgent/emergency care.
If safe, hydrate as appropriate and stand slowly while tracking readings/symptoms.

Get evaluated if fainting or red flags show up.

Type, stage, grade, biomarkers, location, and overall health guide treatment.

Guessing is not diagnosis. The microscope gets a vote.

Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.

biologyPathway.short: Capacity and coordination are still developing.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Developmental Timing
body: Childhood bedwetting is common and often relates to sleep depth, bladder capacity, nighttime urine production, and development of bladder-brain signaling.
It is not laziness, spite, or a child plotting against your laundry basket.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Physical Contributors
body: Constipation, urinary tract infection, sleep issues, family history, and high nighttime urine production can contribute.
Excessive thirst, weight loss, fever, pain with urination, or new daytime accidents need medical attention.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Stress and Safety
body: Stress and family disruption can make bedwetting worse for some children.
Shame increases distress and does not teach the nervous system safety.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Parent Response
body: The adult's job is calm consistency: clean up, reassure, track patterns, address constipation or sleep issues, and involve a clinician when symptoms are new, persistent, painful, or concerning.
You cannot shame a sleeping bladder into maturity. Grim discovery for people who think yelling is a treatment plan.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Night Guard and the Parking Lot
paragraphs: Your child's bladder is a night-shift security guard protecting an empty parking lot.
No one told him the threat is gone. His radio crackles. His coffee is cold. A leaf blows past and he hits the panic button.
The panic button is the bladder signal. The leaf might be a dream, room temperature, constipation pressure, stress, or a body still learning how nighttime works.
The bedwetting is the guard accidentally flooding the parking lot because he confused the flush button with the lights.
It is not malice. It is not laziness. It is a sleepy body system doing a bad impression of competence.
The guard thinks every red mark on the calendar is his fault. Someone needs to tell him the parking lot was never in danger.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The No-Shame Parent Check
paragraphs: This is not about a child being lazy. This is not about a child not trying hard enough.
This is about a sleeping body system, development, bladder signaling, possible constipation, possible stress, and maybe a medical issue that needs checking.
The adult in the room needs to understand this: shame is gasoline. It does not dry the bed. It burns the child.
Every angry cleanup teaches the child that their body is unsafe, embarrassing, and disappointing. That lesson sticks.
Say: it is okay, we will clean it up, you are safe. Then actually mean it.
Track patterns. Check constipation. Watch for pain, thirst, fever, daytime accidents, or sudden changes. Get medical help when needed.
Armor down. The child needs safety more than a lecture.

influenceLayers.title: Kidney-Bladder Security System
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Kidney and Bladder are linked with water regulation and fear.
Symbolically, nighttime wetting can mirror a system that does not yet feel safe enough to rest and hold.

influenceLayers.title: The Unslept Sentinel
paragraphs: The Sentinel is the part that stays alert even when the child sleeps.
The healing pattern is consistency, calm, and repeated proof that night is safe.

influenceLayers.title: Shame Release
paragraphs: A child cannot relax under shame.
The adult response becomes part of the child's nervous system training.

influenceLayers.title: Safe Night Ritual
paragraphs: Predictable bedtime routines can support the body.
The goal is not control through fear. It is trust through repetition.

influenceLayers.title: Blame-Free Repair
paragraphs: Cleanup can become a safety ritual instead of a punishment.
Calm repair tells the child: your body had an accident, not a moral collapse.

reset.title: Night Safety Anchoring
steps: Before sleep, sit with the child if they want company.
Have them place one hand on the belly and one on the chest.
Breathe in together for 4 seconds and out for 8 seconds.
Say: You are safe. Night is safe. If there is an accident, we will clean it up. You are not in trouble.
Repeat nightly without shame.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Adult Bedwetting Needs Evaluation
body: Adult bedwetting is not something to dismiss as laziness or moral failure.
Recurring or new adult bedwetting deserves medical evaluation because many treatable physical causes are possible.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Possible Physical Contributors
body: Possible contributors include urinary tract infection, diabetes, sleep apnea, alcohol, medication effects, prostate or bladder problems, neurologic conditions, seizures, severe constipation, or hormonal/urine production issues.
Daytime urgency, pain, excessive thirst, fever, snoring, medication changes, or neurologic symptoms make evaluation more important.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Stress, Trauma, and Sleep
body: Stress and trauma history can contribute for some people by fragmenting sleep, increasing hyperarousal, or changing the body's nighttime safety signals.
But trauma should not be assumed as the only cause. The adult body deserves actual medical checking, not a dramatic monologue with no lab work.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Shame Reduction
body: Shame is common and deeply unhelpful.
Track the pattern, protect sleep, get evaluated, and treat the body like it is sending a signal rather than betraying you.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Defunct Home Security System
paragraphs: Your bladder is now a defunct home security system installed after an old break-in.
The break-in is over. The burglars are gone. But the system is still blaring every time a mouse sneezes in the wall.
At night, the alarm system glitches. It mistakes bladder fullness, sleep disruption, medication effects, stress, or random nervous-system nonsense for a full emergency.
The bed gets wet. The shame gets louder. The security company sends another bill paid entirely in embarrassment.
But the alarm system is not evil. It is outdated, overtuned, and badly in need of inspection.
Someone needs to check the wiring, the sensors, the sleep system, the fluid schedule, and possibly the fact that Gary from security has been awake since 1997.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Shame Is Not the Treatment
paragraphs: You do not have a character flaw. You have a symptom that needs attention.
Waking up wet as an adult can feel humiliating. It can make you hide, avoid intimacy, avoid doctors, and pretend it was a one-off until it is very obviously not a one-off.
That shame is not helping. Shame is not a diagnostic tool. Shame cannot check your blood sugar, urine, prostate, sleep apnea, medication list, or nervous system.
Get evaluated. Track it. Look at sleep, alcohol, medications, urinary symptoms, thirst, stress, trauma, and timing.
If trauma is part of the picture, handle it with care. If sleep apnea is part of the picture, treat it like oxygen matters, because, tragically, it does.
You cannot punish your body into safety. You investigate, support, and treat it.
Drop the shame costume. Make the appointment before embarrassment starts pretending it is medical advice.

influenceLayers.title: Kidney Reserve
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Kidney is associated with fear, reserves, and water regulation.
Symbolically, adult bedwetting may mirror exhaustion, depleted reserve, and difficulty trusting the night.

influenceLayers.title: Bladder Gatekeeper
paragraphs: The Bladder is the reservoir and release gate.
When the gatekeeper is exhausted or confused, release may happen outside conscious control.

influenceLayers.title: The Exhausted Gatekeeper
paragraphs: The Gatekeeper was built to protect.
When it never gets relief, it may fail not from weakness but from exhaustion.

influenceLayers.title: Night Safety
paragraphs: For some people, night carries old fear.
The symbolic reset is teaching the body: I am not there anymore. Night is not the old threat.

influenceLayers.title: Disarming Shame
paragraphs: The symptom asks for care, not disgust.
Medical evaluation and emotional safety can work together without pretending one replaces the other.

reset.title: Night Safety & Medical Tracking Reset
steps: Before sleep, lie down with one hand on the lower belly and one hand on the chest.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
On each exhale, say: I am not there anymore. I am safe now. Night is safe. I can rest.
Afterward, track sleep, fluids, alcohol, medications, urinary symptoms, and timing for medical discussion.

Do not assume it is only trauma or only stress.

Check sleep, alcohol, medications, thirst, urinary symptoms, and stress.
Book medical evaluation if recurring or new.
Stop letting shame pretend it is a treatment plan.

clinical.label: Strictly Medical
clinical.title: Cyst Type, Location, Complication & Evaluation Pathway

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Cyst Is a Structure, Not a Single Diagnosis
body: A cyst is a closed sac-like structure that may contain fluid, air, or semi-solid material.
The meaning and risk depend heavily on where it is and what type it is.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Common Types
body: Common types include epidermoid/skin cysts, ovarian cysts, breast cysts, ganglion cysts, pilonidal cysts, Baker’s cysts, renal cysts, and PCOS-related ovarian findings.
This is why 'I have a cyst' is not enough information. The cyst apparently wants a zip code.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Treatment Direction
body: Treatment may include monitoring, imaging, drainage, antibiotics if infected, hormonal management, aspiration, biopsy, or surgical removal.
Do not squeeze or cut cysts at home, unless your goal is turning a bump into a medical regret.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: Seek urgent care for fever, spreading redness/warmth, severe pain, rapid swelling, pus, sudden severe pelvic pain, nausea/vomiting/faintness, or neurologic symptoms.
Any new breast lump needs medical evaluation, even if you suspect a cyst.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Water Balloon
paragraphs: Your body made a water balloon in a place water balloons do not usually belong.
Sometimes it is harmless. Sometimes it gets infected. Sometimes it ruptures. Sometimes it sits on an ovary and starts acting like a tiny emergency goblin.
The answer is not kitchen-counter surgery.
The answer is identifying the type, location, risk, and right treatment.
Release the pressure safely or keep treating a sealed pocket like it owes you an explanation. Your choice.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Containment Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have a cyst problem. You may also have a containment-and-release pattern.
Medical first: cyst risk depends on location, size, symptoms, infection signs, rupture, torsion risk, breast changes, and whether it is growing or suspicious.
Symbolically, ask what you keep holding in a pocket.
But do not make the cyst your fault. Bodies make pockets for reasons ranging from boring to serious.
Release the pressure safely or keep treating a sealed pocket like it owes you an explanation. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen Dampness / Liver Stagnation
paragraphs: In TCM imagery, cysts can be mapped to dampness, stagnation, and accumulated fluids.
Medically, cyst type and location are the actual decision-makers.

influenceLayers.title: Pocket Self
paragraphs: The Pocket Self stores what feels unsafe to release.
The Releaser learns flow without self-blame.

influenceLayers.title: Water Balloon
paragraphs: The water balloon image captures contained pressure.
Pop it yourself? No. Let people with sterile tools and training handle the balloon circus.

influenceLayers.title: Boundary Wall
paragraphs: A cyst wall holds contents apart from surrounding tissue.
Symbolically, the wall asks what has been separated off.

influenceLayers.title: Safe Release
paragraphs: The reset is evaluation, safe treatment, and emotional release without blame.
No bathroom surgery. Civilization has dentists and doctors for a reason.

reset.title: Pocket Release & Safe Evaluation Reset
steps: Sit comfortably and do not press hard on the cyst.
Place one hand nearby, not forcefully on it, and one hand on the chest.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale slowly for 8 seconds.
Think: I release what I can emotionally, and I get the pocket checked medically when needed.

items.detail: Ultrasound, mammogram, MRI/CT, or other imaging may be used depending on location.

items.label: Do not squeeze
items.detail: Squeezing can worsen inflammation or infection and fail to remove the cyst wall.

avoid: Do not ignore fever, spreading redness/warmth, severe pain, rapid swelling, pus, sudden pelvic pain, vomiting, faintness, new breast lump, or neurologic symptoms.
Do not cut, burn, lance, squeeze, or drain cysts at home.

brutalActions: Identify location and red flags.
Stop poking it.
Schedule evaluation if new/growing/painful/suspicious.
Release one thing emotionally without blaming your body for building a pocket.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Depression Is Medical
body: Depression is a serious mood disorder, not weakness, laziness, or a bad attitude.
Symptoms can affect mood, sleep, appetite, energy, thinking, movement, pain, and functioning.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Causes and Contributors
body: Depression can involve genetic, biological, trauma-related, medication-related, medical, substance-related, sleep-related, and social contributors.
A single cause explanation is usually too small for a disorder this obnoxiously layered.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Treatment Options
body: Treatment may include therapy, medication, support groups, exercise as tolerated, sleep treatment, medical review, substance care, and structured daily support.
Severe or treatment-resistant depression may require specialist care and other treatments.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: If there are thoughts of suicide or self-harm, plan/intent, inability to stay safe, psychosis, mania, severe neglect, or danger to self/others, seek immediate emergency/crisis help.
Depression can lie. Safety gets priority over the lie.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Dimmer Switch
paragraphs: Your life is a room and the dimmer switch is stuck on low.
People keep shouting just turn on the lights, as if the switch is not broken, the wiring is not fried, and the electric company is not run by raccoons.
The fix is not shame.
The fix is treatment, support, sleep, food, routine, medical review, and sometimes medication or therapy because the room deserves electricity.
Turn the dimmer up with real support or keep blaming yourself for a room with broken wiring. Your choice.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest

paragraphs: You may have depression. You may also have a depletion-and-disconnection pattern.
Medical first: depression is treatable and serious. Safety comes first when self-harm or suicidal thoughts appear.
Symbolically, ask what has drained you, but do not turn depression into a moral failure.
You are not weak for needing help. You are a human nervous system, not a motivational poster with legs.
Turn the dimmer up with real support or keep blaming yourself for a room with broken wiring. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Heart-Kidney Depletion

The Depleted Self learned to run on empty.

The wiring can be supported.

influenceLayers.title: Light Return

The light returns by degrees, not by bullying.

reset.title: Dimmer Switch & Safety-First Mood Reset

If unsafe, seek immediate emergency/crisis support or contact a trusted person now.
If safe, sit with both feet on the floor.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds and exhale for 8 seconds.
Think: I do not have to fix my whole life today. I take one safe next step.

Book or keep mental-health/medical care.
Do one tiny body-maintenance action before negotiating with hopelessness.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Side Is a Clue, Not a Diagnosis
body: Left-sided symptoms can come from many systems.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Meaning Layer
body: Symbolically, the left side is often described as receptive, emotional, or yin/feminine in some traditions.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty

Fix the left tire safely or keep driving a one-sided car into medical traffic. Your choice.

biologyPathway.title: Clostridium tetani
biologyPathway.detail: Tetanus bacteria can enter through punctures, contaminated wounds, burns, crush injuries, animal bites, or wounds exposed to soil/dust/manure.

biologyPathway.title: Toxin Blocks Brakes
biologyPathway.detail: The toxin interferes with inhibitory nerve signaling, causing severe involuntary muscle contractions.

biologyPathway.title: Lockjaw and Spasms
biologyPathway.short: Jaw stiffness can be an early sign.
biologyPathway.detail: Symptoms may include jaw stiffness, trouble swallowing, neck/back/abdominal rigidity, painful spasms, sweating, high blood pressure, and breathing trouble.

biologyPathway.title: Vaccination Prevents
biologyPathway.detail: Vaccination and proper wound management prevent tetanus. Natural infection does not reliably protect you, because biology loves cruel jokes.

clinical.label: Strictly Medical
clinical.title: Tetanus Toxin & Loss of Nervous-System Brakes

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Emergency Infection
body: Tetanus is a potentially fatal bacterial toxin disease.
It is not a stress cramp and not a mind-body pattern.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Brake Failure
body: The toxin blocks inhibitory nerve signals, causing severe spasms and rigidity.
Jaw stiffness, trouble swallowing, and painful spasms are major warning signs.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Hospital Treatment
body: Treatment can require hospitalization, wound care, tetanus immune globulin, antibiotics, sedating/muscle-relaxing medications, and breathing support.
This is not backyard medicine unless the backyard contains an ICU, which it does not.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Prevention
body: Vaccination boosters and proper wound care prevent tetanus.
Dirty, deep, puncture, crush, burn, or animal-bite wounds may require medical review.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Overridden Brakes
paragraphs: Your nervous system is a car whose brake lines have been cut.
The gas pedal is stuck. The driver is screaming stop. The muscles are not taking notes.
That is tetanus. Not tension. Not spiritual rigidity. Not a Pilates opportunity.
The reset protocol is emergency care.
The prevention protocol is vaccination and wound care.
No one wins a debate with tetanospasmin. The toxin does not attend workshops.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Medical Emergency Reality Check
paragraphs: You do not have a metaphor problem. You may have a life-threatening emergency.
Tetanus can kill. Jaw stiffness, painful spasms, trouble swallowing, trouble breathing, neck/back rigidity, or symptoms after a contaminated wound need emergency care.
Do not search for home remedies.
Do not wait to see if it passes.
Get emergency care. Get vaccinated. Clean wounds properly.
This is survival, not symbolism.

For future prevention, keep tetanus vaccination current.

brutalActions: Say one clean no.
Ask one person for help.
Return one task to its owner.
Stop turning exhaustion into your personality résumé.

clinical.mechanism: A martyr complex is not a clinical diagnosis. It is a behavioral and psychological pattern involving chronic self-sacrifice, over-giving, guilt around self-care, poor boundaries, resentment, burnout risk, anxiety, depression, and physical stress symptoms. Chronic caregiving or over-functioning without support can worsen exhaustion and health strain.
protocol: Track burnout, resentment, guilt when saying no, sleep, mood, anxiety, health symptoms, caregiving load, lack of reciprocity, self-harm thoughts, suicidal thoughts, and whether therapy/support is available.
Use medical, neurologic, mental-health, or emergency evaluation when symptoms are sudden, severe, progressive, persistent, recurrent, or paired with red flags.
Symbolic interpretation never replaces diagnosis, emergency care, therapy, or treatment.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for professional care.

witty.metaphorText: You are the donkey and everyone keeps adding luggage.
witty.wittyAdvice: Unload the donkey or keep letting every suitcase prove you exist. Your choice.

brutal.realityCheck: You may have a martyr pattern. You may also have a worth-through-usefulness pattern.
protocolSteps: Get support if burnout/depression is present.

critical_alerts: Thoughts of self-harm, suicide, death, or not wanting to live
Severe depression, panic, inability to function, or feeling emotionally unsafe
Caregiver collapse, neglect of basic needs, or severe burnout
Domestic violence, coercion, exploitation, or unsafe dependency dynamics
Physical symptoms from exhaustion, chest pain, fainting, severe insomnia, or substance crisis
seek_immediate_care_if: Self-harm or suicidal thoughts include plan, intent, means, or inability to stay safe
You are in immediate danger, being abused, or cannot care for basic safety
schedule_care_if: Over-giving, guilt, resentment, burnout, depression, anxiety, or boundary collapse persists
Therapy, caregiver support, medical evaluation, or crisis planning is needed
medical_safety.self_care_allowed_if: Rest, boundaries, asking for help, journaling, therapy, and caregiver respite can support this pattern.
medical_safety.do_not_ignore: Chronic self-sacrifice can become burnout, depression, physical illness, or safety risk.
medical_safety.disclaimer: This app is for education and self-reflection only. It does not diagnose, treat, prevent, or cure mental-health conditions.
